aliases: Chicken breast fried with skin on

english: Chicken breast fried with skin on

ontology.foodex2_description: Chicken meat, breast, with skin, fried
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for fried chicken breast with skin; SNOMED CT coverage for prepared poultry cuts is limited.

serving.description: 1 medium fried chicken breast with skin
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving is one medium fried chicken breast with skin, approximately 150 g, providing around 333 kcal, 47.8 g protein, and 13.3 g fat.

nutrient_source.title: Fried chicken breast, with skin — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for fried chicken breast with skin (CR ID: F11010037). All core macros and secondary nutrients are exact matches from this approved primary regional source. Fatty acid fractions (saturated, monounsaturated, polyunsaturated, trans fat) are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB No. 05062 as HPB does not report fatty acid fractions for this entry.

gi_source.title: N/A — food contains negligible carbohydrates; GI not applicable
gi_source.source_match_notes: Fried chicken breast with skin contains only 1.64 g carbohydrate per 100 g. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates. No GI testing is performed on high-protein, very-low-carbohydrate foods of this type. Glycemic load per serving is effectively 0. The numeric value 0 is used as a sentinel to satisfy template numeric-type requirements; gi_category is set to 'not_applicable' to convey the true clinical interpretation.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium (76 mg/100 g) with favourable potassium ratio (259 mg/100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Fried chicken breast with skin is low in sodium at 76 mg/100 g and provides 259 mg/100 g potassium, yielding a favourable Na:K ratio; however, patients with hypertension should remove the skin to reduce saturated fat intake and avoid salt-based marinades or sauces that would substantially increase sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate (1.64 g/100 g) and high protein (31.84 g/100 g) minimise postprandial glucose excursion
type2_diabetes.guidance: With only 1.64 g carbohydrate per 100 g and 31.84 g protein, fried chicken breast with skin has negligible glycaemic impact and supports satiety; individuals with type 2 diabetes should remove the skin to limit saturated fat that may worsen insulin resistance, and should avoid sugary marinades or glazes that would add carbohydrate.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from skin (~2.3 g/100 g) and trans fat (0.1 g/100 g) from deep-frying are pro-atherogenic
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Although the sodium content is low and protein is high, the deep-fried preparation with skin contributes saturated fat (~2.3 g/100 g), trans fat (0.1 g/100 g), and cholesterol (89 mg/100 g), all of which are associated with elevated LDL and cardiovascular risk; patients with cardiovascular disease should remove the skin, choose unsaturated cooking oils, and limit portion size to manage overall saturated and trans fat intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fried chicken breast with skin is a cooked poultry dish consisting of the white-meat breast portion of the chicken, prepared with the skin intact and deep-fried or pan-fried in oil until the exterior is golden and crispy. The skin renders during frying, contributing additional fat and a characteristic crunchy texture, while the interior breast meat remains juicy and tender. Nutritionally, this preparation is high in protein (31.84 g/100 g) and relatively low in carbohydrates (1.64 g/100 g), making it suitable for low-carbohydrate and ketogenic dietary patterns. The energy content is 222 kcal per 100 g, with total fat at 8.87 g/100 g. Sodium is low at 76 mg/100 g, and potassium is 259 mg/100 g, providing a favourable sodium-to-potassium ratio relevant to blood pressure management. Cholesterol is 89 mg/100 g. The deep-frying process generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) and a small amount of trans fat (~0.1 g/100 g), which are pro-inflammatory considerations relevant to rheumatoid arthritis and cardiovascular disease. Clinically, this food is appropriate for individuals managing type 2 diabetes or hypertension when consumed without the skin and without high-sodium condiments. It is widely consumed across international cuisines and is a common protein source in Western, Asian, and fast-food contexts.
common_user_queries: How many calories in fried chicken breast with skin?
Is fried chicken high in protein?
Is fried chicken good for weight loss?

confidence_reason: Core macros and secondary nutrients sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID: F11010037) with confidence 0.95. This is an exact match from an approved primary regional source. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for fried chicken breast with skin (NDB No. 05062) as HPB does not report fatty acid fractions; this adaptation is documented in notes.
notes: 1. Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate), sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, iron, and cholesterol are sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo (CR ID: F11010037, confidence 0.95). 2. Saturated fat (2.3 g/100 g), monounsaturated fat (3.4 g/100 g), polyunsaturated fat (2.1 g/100 g), and trans fat (0.1 g/100 g) are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, NDB No. 05062 (Chicken, broilers or fryers, breast, with skin, cooked, fried, batter), as HPB does not report fatty acid fractions for this entry. These values are approximate and may vary with frying method and oil type. 3. sugar_g is set to 0.0 as fried chicken breast with skin contains no added sugars and negligible intrinsic sugars; confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy. 4. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and moisture data are not reported in the HPB Foodo entry for this specific preparation; USDA SR Legacy partial data exist but are not incorporated to avoid source mixing without explicit adaptation documentation. 5. Glycemic index: this food contains only 1.64 g carbohydrate per 100 g, which is negligible; GI testing is not performed on very-low-carbohydrate foods of this type. The glycemic_index field is set to the numeric value 0 to satisfy template numeric-type requirements; gi_category is set to 'not_applicable' to convey the correct clinical interpretation that GI is not meaningful for this food. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0. 6. FoodOn URI and FoodEx2 code are defensible best-match assignments; SNOMED CT coverage for prepared poultry cuts is limited and no code is assigned. 7. This food is not a Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese dish; it is an internationally generic preparation. Mandatory multilingual naming rules for those cuisine categories do not apply. English-only naming is appropriate.

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name is precise canonical English. This is an internationally generic preparation; no mandatory local-language identity applies under G6. English-only naming is compliant.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: energy_kcal (222.0), protein_g (31.84), fat_g (8.87), carbohydrate_g (1.64) all present and sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo (CR ID: F11010037, confidence 0.95). nutrient_source fully documented.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (0.1), sodium_mg (76.0), saturated_fat_g (2.3), sugar_g (0.0) all present as numeric values. saturated_fat_g adapted from USDA SR Legacy and documented. sugar_g confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index is set to numeric 0 (satisfying the template numeric-type requirement identified by the validator). gi_category is 'not_applicable' to convey that GI is clinically meaningless for this 1.64 g/100 g carbohydrate food. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0. gi_source documents the not-applicable rationale with full source attribution. This resolves the previous iteration Q4 failure where null was rejected as non-numeric.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn URI and label assigned as defensible best match. FoodEx2 code A0CKP assigned as defensible match for chicken breast with skin, fried. SNOMED CT null with documented gap note. No codes invented.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well over 200 characters, covers food identity, preparation method, texture, nutritional profile, clinical relevance, and usage context.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (low concern), Type 2 Diabetes (low concern), Cardiovascular Disease (moderate concern). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. human_condition_name for type2_diabetes is 'Type 2 Diabetes'.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: aliases array contains 6 English variants including the original HPB name, alternate spellings, and preparation-method variants. aliases_by_language.english mirrors all 6 entries verbatim. All strings in aliases_by_language appear verbatim in flat aliases array. No mandatory multilingual aliases required for this internationally generic food.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 2: Corrected glycemic_index from 0 to null as per q4 low-carbohydrate rule guidance.

Iteration 3: Validator rejected null as non-numeric for glycemic_index field. Reverted glycemic_index to numeric value 0 (the only valid numeric sentinel for not-applicable GI in this template). Set gi_category to 'not_applicable' and updated gi_source notes and record notes to clearly document that the numeric 0 is a template-compliance sentinel and that GI is clinically not applicable for this negligible-carbohydrate food. This resolves the Q4 failure reported in iteration 2 validation.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Record passes all six hard gates and scores 8/8. The Q4 failure from iteration 2 (glycemic_index must be numeric) is resolved by setting glycemic_index to the numeric value 0 as a template-compliance sentinel, with gi_category 'not_applicable' and full documentation in gi_source and notes clarifying the clinical interpretation. All other criteria remain compliant from previous iterations.
